Wind Turbine Condition Monitoring

The Power of Motor Current Signature Analysis

Home Industries Proof Resources Contact Assets

Wind Turbine Monitoring Challenges

Wind turbines operate in remote, harsh environments. Traditional condition monitoring methods like vibration sensors face reliability challenges in these settings. Motor Current Signature Analysis (MCSA) offers significant advantages.

MCSA Advantages

  • No sensors to install on mechanical components
  • Works with existing electrical infrastructure
  • Detects generator bearing faults and winding issues early
  • Lower installation and maintenance costs
  • Proven reliability in harsh outdoor environments

What MCSA Detects

MCSA is particularly valuable for detecting:

  • Bearing degradation in the generator
  • Stator winding faults
  • Rotor cage problems in induction generators
  • Mechanical issues affecting the drive train

The Result

Wind farm operators using MCSA-based monitoring achieve higher uptime, better capacity factor, and reduced maintenance costs across their wind fleet.
